reasoning: The value on 2025-10-26 is 8.7°C. We need the probability that the daily average temperature at Alençon will be strictly greater than 8.7°C on Nov 2, Nov 25, and Jan 24. Climatologically, the average daily mean temperature in Alençon falls from around 9-10°C in early November to around 6°C by late November, and approximately 4.5°C in late January. Therefore, the threshold of 8.7°C is very close to the expected mean for our first target date (Nov 2), but substantially higher than the expected mean for the later dates. Given the absence of short-term weather model forecasts, we rely on climatological distributions. Early November has about a ~55% chance of exceeding 8.7°C. Late November expects a mean of ~6°C with a standard deviation around 3.5°C, resulting in a ~20-30% chance. Late January expects a mean of ~4.5°C, yielding roughly a 15-25% chance of anomalous warmth exceeding 8.7°C. I progressively soften these climatological probabilities towards 0.5 to properly account for forecasting uncertainty over longer time horizons.
batch_queries: What is the probability that the daily average temperature at the French weather station at Alençon will be higher on each resolution date listed below than on 2025-10-26?
